llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI/computer arm in the study. This is a natural experiment comparing workers with vs. without computer access.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a natural experiment using a difference-in-differences design. The primary outcome is log(tasks completed per hour). The paper reports a regression coefficient of 0.2608 (SE=0.056) for computer access, which represents a 26.08% increase in productivity. The outcome is log-transformed, so the coefficient directly represents the log ratio. The 95% CI is [15.08%, 37.08%]. Sample sizes are not reported in traditional arm format because this is an observational study with regression analysis, but the total sample is 5,041 workers with 241,854 worker-day observations.
source_quotes: Workers with computer access complete 26.08% more tasks per hour (95% CI: [15.08%, 37.08%])
The dependent variable is log(tasks completed per hour)
Our sample consists of 5,041 workers contributing 241,854 worker-day observations

computed.notes: This is a natural experiment using a difference-in-differences design. The primary outcome is log(tasks completed per hour). The paper reports a regression coefficient of 0.2608 (SE=0.056) for computer access, which represents a 26.08% increase in productivity. The outcome is log-transformed, so the coefficient directly represents the log ratio. The 95% CI is [15.08%, 37.08%]. Sample sizes are not reported in traditional arm format because this is an observational study with regression analysis, but the total sample is 5,041 workers with 241,854 worker-day observations.
